entities.name: MARKET PARTICIPATION

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The efficiency and output levels of farm households

entities.name: LIVELIHOODS
entities.description: The means of securing basic necessities and improving the quality of life for farm households

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of growing crops, which is noted as risky in drylands

entities.name: LIVESTOCK MARKETS
entities.description: Markets specifically for the trade of livestock animals and products

entities.name: LIVESTOCK PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of raising livestock, which can be market-oriented and supports food security

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to sufficient, affordable, and nutritious food

entities.name: ZIMBABWE
entities.description: The country where the study on smallholder livestock market participation was conducted

entities.name: SURVEY DATA
entities.description: Data collected from 625 households used for empirical analysis

entities.name: INSTRUMENTAL VARIABLES STRATEGY
entities.description: A statistical approach used to address endogeneity concerns in the research

entities.name: ENDOGENEITY
entities.description: A statistical concern where an explanatory variable is correlated with the error term

entities.name: LIVESTOCK INPUTS
entities.description: Improved resources used to enhance the efficiency of livestock production

entities.name: MARKET CONDITIONS
entities.description: External economic factors that influence the production decisions of market participants

entities.name: DRYLANDS
